title: Anthraquinones Group - Canada.ca
content:
## Anthraquinones Group
- Anthraquinones Group (information sheet)
- Solvent 13 (consumer information)
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). This screening assessment focuses on 7 of 15 substances referred to collectively under  the CMP as the as the Anthraquinones Group.
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities
Proposed order and RIAS
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Proposed risk management approach
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Anthraquinones Group
9,10-Anthracenedione, 1-hydroxy-4-[(4-methylphenyl)amino]-
Meets one or more of the criteria
Refer to proposed risk management approach
5,9,14,18-Anthrazinetetrone, 6,15-dihydro-
None planned at this time
9,10-Anthracenedione, 1,4-diamino-2,3-diphenoxy-
9,10-Anthracenedione, 1,4-bis[(1-methylethyl)amino]-
9,10-Anthracenedione, 1-amino-4-hydroxy-2-phenoxy-
Benzenesulfonic acid, [[(chloroacetyl)amino]methyl][4-[[4-(cyclohexylamino)-9,10-dihydro-9,10-dioxo-1-anthracenyl]amino]phenoxy]methyl-, monosodium salt
9,10-Anthracenedione, 1,4-diamino-, N,N'-mixed 2-ethylhexyl and Me and pentyl derivs.

## Follow-up activities
Although a risk to human health or the environment has not been identified at current levels of exposure, there may be a concern if exposure to Solvent Violet 59, Solvent Blue 36, Disperse Red 60, Acid Blue 239, and CAS RN 74499-36-8
  were to increase. As a result, these substances may be considered in future initiatives to track their commercial status or identify new uses.
Publication  in the Canada Gazette, Part I, Vol. 156. No. 40 of a proposed order adding Solvent Violet 13 to  Schedule 1 to the Canadian Environmental  Protection Act, 1999, and start of a 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the Final Screening Assessment for the Anthraquinones Group . The Risk Management Approach for Solvent Violet 13 was also published for a 60-day public comment period. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I, Vol. 155, No. 29 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the Draft Screening Assessment for the Anthraquinones Group (including follow-up activities for Solvent Violet 59, Solvent Blue 36, Disperse Red 60, Acid Blue 239, and CAS RN 74499-36-8) and the Risk Management Scope for Solvent Violet 13 . The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 44 .
Eight other substances, CAS RNs 2475-45-8, 4051-63-2, 13676-91-0, 19286-75-0, 2379-79-5, 15791-78-3, 19720-45-7 and 28173-59-3, were identified as being part of the Anthraquinones Group at the outset of the third phase of the CMP . These substances were subsequently determined to be of low concern to both human health and the environment, through other approaches. Conclusions for CAS RNs 2379-79-5, 15791-78-3, 19720-45-7 and 28173-59-3 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment of Substances Identified as Being of Low Concern using the Ecological Risk Classification of Organic Substances and the Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC)-based Approach for Certain Substances . Conclusions for CAS RNs 2475-45-8, 4051-63-2, 13676-91-0 and 19286-75-0 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for the Rapid Screening of Substances with Limited General Population Exposure .
